blurb: Plans and maintains B.C.'s transportation network — highways, bridges, public transit, BC Ferries oversight, and commercial vehicle safety.

goals.title: GOAL 1
goals.short_name: British Columbians travel safely on well-maintained highways and roads
goals.description: The ministry builds, maintains and permits use of B.C.'s provincial highway network, and keeps travellers informed about conditions, closures and construction.

supporting.name: Check road conditions before you travel
supporting.desc: DriveBC gives real-time road conditions, closures, delays and highway webcams so travellers can plan a route before setting out, including conditions for inland ferry crossings.

relationships.service: Check your route and restrictions before you drive a commercial vehicle
Get verified emergency information during an active disaster
Check ferry conditions and closures before you travel

supporting.name: Report a highway problem
supporting.desc: Report potholes, debris, signal outages and other highway problems through DriveBC so maintenance crews can respond.

main_services.name: Get a permit to build, access or use a provincial highway

supporting.name: Apply for a highway access permit for commercial or development access
supporting.desc: Local governments, developers and businesses need a highway use permit to create or change commercial or development access off a provincial highway, including a traffic impact study for larger developments.

channels.label: PASport - apply for a highway use permit
Form H0020 Provincial Public Highway Permit Application

legislation.full_name: Transportation Act - Minister's rights, powers and advantages over highway right-of-way

supporting.name: Apply for a highway access permit for a rural home or farm driveway
supporting.desc: Property owners must apply for a highway use permit to create or change a driveway off a provincially maintained controlled-access or numbered highway, including access for large farm equipment reaching fields or barns. A permit is not needed for a single-family driveway off an ordinary side road outside a municipal boundary.

channels.label: PASport - apply for a highway use permit

legislation.full_name: Transportation Act - Minister's rights, powers and advantages over highway right-of-way

supporting.name: Apply for a highway works permit to build or repair near a highway
supporting.desc: A highway use permit is needed to construct, repair or maintain works — such as utility lines, drainage or signage — on or along a provincial highway right-of-way.

channels.label: PASport - apply for a highway use permit

legislation.full_name: Transportation Act - Minister's rights, powers and advantages over highway right-of-way

supporting.name: Apply for a highway event permit
supporting.desc: Organizers need a highway use permit to hold an event, such as a race, parade or filming activity, on or along a provincial highway right-of-way.

channels.label: PASport - apply for a highway use permit

legislation.full_name: Transportation Act - Minister's rights, powers and advantages over highway right-of-way

supporting.name: Get a subdivision development referral for rural land near a highway
supporting.desc: Developers and local governments proposing a rural subdivision use the Permits, Approvals and Subdivisions Portal (PASport) to get the ministry's referral on highway access and traffic impacts before subdivision approval.

legislation.full_name: Transportation Act - Minister's rights, powers and advantages over highway right-of-way

main_services.name: Follow transportation infrastructure projects near you

supporting.name: Find transportation and highway improvement projects in your area
supporting.desc: Information on transportation and highway infrastructure projects underway across the province and how they may affect travel.

mandate_tags.text: Support improvements in BC's road infrastructure balanced with integrated transit opportunities to ensure that people can get home and to work faster, and goods can get to market more efficiently in our province

goals.title: GOAL 2
goals.short_name: Commercial vehicles and goods move safely and efficiently across B.C.
goals.description: The ministry permits, inspects and regulates commercial trucking through Commercial Vehicle Safety and Enforcement (CVSE), keeping goods moving safely on B.C. highways.

main_services.name: Get a permit for your commercial vehicle or load

supporting.name: Apply for an oversize or overweight load permit
supporting.desc: Term and single-trip permits for commercial loads that exceed standard weight or dimension limits, applied for online through onRouteBC or Commercial Vehicle Permits Online.

supporting.name: Apply for an extraordinary load approval
supporting.desc: Special authorizations and route approvals for extraordinary loads that exceed regular permit weight, width, height or length limits, coordinated with local authorities and utilities along the route.

supporting.name: Get a motive fuel user permit for your commercial vehicle
supporting.desc: A temporary permit purchased by an owner or operator of a fuel-powered commercial vehicle entering B.C. that is not currently registered under the International Fuel Tax Agreement (IFTA).

supporting.name: Check your route and restrictions before you drive a commercial vehicle
supporting.desc: The Commercial Vehicle Routing Tool shows known route, bridge and dimension restrictions for commercial vehicles, since general mapping services are not accurate for truck routing in B.C.

relationships.service: Check road conditions before you travel

supporting.name: Get a National Safety Code safety certificate for your fleet
supporting.desc: Commercial carriers operating in B.C. must hold a National Safety Code safety certificate, tracking each carrier's safety record and compliance history.

supporting.name: Get your commercial vehicle inspected
supporting.desc: Mandatory and on-demand safety inspections for commercial trucks, trailers and buses operating on B.C. highways.

supporting.name: Join the Weigh2GoBC weigh-scale bypass program
supporting.desc: Free program that lets registered commercial carriers bypass weigh-in-motion inspection stations, saving time and fuel while letting CVSE focus roadside checks on higher-risk carriers.

supporting.name: Apply to become a recognized Premium Carrier
supporting.desc: The Premium Carrier Program recognizes and rewards commercial carriers with an exceptional safety record, offering reduced random inspections and free Weigh2GoBC transponders for their fleet.
supporting.users: Trucking companies with strong safety records

goals.title: GOAL 3
goals.short_name: People have convenient, accessible ways to get around B.C.
goals.description: The ministry oversees public transit, licenses taxi, limousine and ride-hail services, and connects communities by ferry, air and rail.

supporting.name: Find BC Transit routes and schedules in your community
supporting.desc: BC Transit connects over 50 million customer trips a year in communities across the province, from small towns to large urban centres.

legislation.full_name: British Columbia Transit Act - Continuation and purposes of British Columbia Transit

mandate_tags.text: Find ways to support low-income people including seniors and young people in accessing affordable transit

supporting.name: Find TransLink routes and schedules in Metro Vancouver
supporting.desc: TransLink operates the integrated bus, SkyTrain, SeaBus and West Coast Express network across Metro Vancouver.

legislation.full_name: South Coast British Columbia Transportation Authority Act - Establishment of the transportation authority

supporting.name: Get accessible door-to-door transit through HandyDART
supporting.desc: HandyDART is an accessible, door-to-door shared transit service for people whose disability prevents them from using fixed-route transit without assistance.

channels.label: Find HandyDART service in your area

legislation.full_name: British Columbia Transit Act - Continuation and purposes of British Columbia Transit

mandate_tags.text: Ensure that our provincial transit services are being delivered in a way that is cost-effective for taxpayers, responsive to the concerns of transit riders, and not duplicative of administration by reviewing the private delivery model for provincial transit systems starting with handyDART

supporting.name: Travel long-distance by BC Bus North coach service
supporting.desc: BC Bus North offers fixed-route, scheduled coach service connecting Prince Rupert, Prince George, Dawson Creek, Fort St. John, Fort Nelson and Valemount.

channels.label: BC Bus North schedules and fares

mandate_tags.text: Drive the development and expansion of transit across the province and work with communities across BC to find ways to strengthen key rural and intercity transportation services

supporting.name: Dispute a TransLink fare evasion ticket
supporting.desc: Process for disputing a fare evasion fine issued on the TransLink transit system.

legislation.full_name: South Coast British Columbia Transportation Authority Act - Establishment of the transportation authority

main_services.name: Use a licensed taxi, limousine or ride-hail service

supporting.name: Find a licensed taxi or ride-hail company in your area
supporting.desc: The Passenger Transportation Registry lists licensed taxi, limousine, shuttle and ride-hail operators approved by the Passenger Transportation Board, and the regions where ride-hail companies can operate.

legislation.full_name: Passenger Transportation Act - Registrar of Passenger Transportation and licensee records

mandate_tags.text: Find ways to support taxi and ride hail operators and ensure safe and affordable transportation options for British Columbians

supporting.name: Know your rights as a taxi passenger
supporting.desc: The Taxi Bill of Rights sets out what passengers can expect from drivers, and what drivers can expect from passengers, on a taxi trip.

channels.label: B.C.'s Voluntary Taxi Bill of Rights

supporting.name: Apply for a passenger transportation licence to operate a taxi, limo or ride-hail business
supporting.desc: Operators of taxis, limousines, shuttles, inter-city buses and ride-hail (transportation network) services need a licence or temporary operating permit from the Registrar of Passenger Transportation or the Passenger Transportation Board.

legislation.full_name: Passenger Transportation Act - Licences and temporary operating permits under Part 4

mandate_tags.text: Find ways to support taxi and ride hail operators and ensure safe and affordable transportation options for British Columbians

supporting.name: Find requirements to drive a taxi, limousine or ride-hail vehicle
supporting.desc: Driver requirements — including screening and vehicle identifier rules — for people driving passenger-directed vehicles such as taxis, limousines and ride-hail vehicles in B.C.

legislation.full_name: Passenger Transportation Act - Vehicle identifiers and driver requirements

main_services.name: Travel by ferry, air or rail

supporting.name: Check inland ferry schedules, capacity and crossing times
supporting.desc: B.C.'s 14 free inland ferries — including the Kootenay Lake, Francois Lake and Lytton reaction ferries — operate under contract with the ministry, with schedule, crossing time and vehicle capacity information for each crossing.

supporting.name: Check ferry conditions and closures before you travel
supporting.desc: Inland ferry service closures, interruptions and delays are posted on DriveBC alongside road conditions.

relationships.service: Check road conditions before you travel

supporting.name: Track BC Ferries fares and service levels
supporting.desc: The province funds BC Ferries through the Coastal Ferry Services Contract, which sets routes, service levels and fare-affordability commitments for the independent, publicly-accountable ferry operator.

mandate_tags.text: Work with BC ferries to address administrative costs and ensure affordable, reliable, and sustainable ferry services

supporting.name: Get information on air and rail travel in B.C.
supporting.desc: Air and rail links are key to connecting remote B.C. communities to the rest of the province and beyond, including regional airports and passenger rail service.
supporting.users: Travellers in remote and rural communities

supporting.name: Get transit and highway access along Highway 16
supporting.desc: Public transportation services are available to residents and travellers along Highway 16 between Prince Rupert and Prince George.

goals.title: GOAL 4
goals.short_name: Communities get funding to build safer, more connected transportation options
goals.description: The ministry cost-shares infrastructure investments with local governments, Indigenous communities and the taxi industry to expand active transportation, transit and aviation infrastructure.

supporting.name: Apply for active transportation infrastructure grants
supporting.desc: Cost-sharing grants help Indigenous and local governments build multi-use paths, bike lanes and other active transportation infrastructure for all ages and abilities.

channels.label: B.C. Active Transportation Infrastructure Grants Program

supporting.name: Apply for an active transportation network planning grant
supporting.desc: Cost-sharing grants help smaller communities with populations under 25,000 create or update an active transportation network plan.

supporting.name: Apply for transit minor betterments funding
supporting.desc: Funding of up to $100,000 for transit-supportive projects such as bus stop improvements, transit shelters and accessibility upgrades.

supporting.name: Apply for passenger transportation accessibility program funding
supporting.desc: The Passenger Transportation Accessibility Program (PTAP) funds and trains the taxi industry to improve services and experiences for the accessibility community.

supporting.name: Apply for aviation infrastructure funding
supporting.desc: Cost-sharing programs support community aviation facility projects such as runway extensions, terminal expansions and lighting and navigational system upgrades.

foundational_services.name: Provincial highway maintenance contract management
foundational_services.desc: Manages performance-based highway maintenance contracts with private contractors across B.C.'s highway maintenance service areas.

foundational_services.name: Commercial Vehicle Safety and Enforcement roadside operations
foundational_services.desc: Roadside inspection, weigh scale operations and compliance enforcement for commercial vehicles under the National Safety Code.

foundational_services.name: Ministry of Transportation and Transit program and initiative review
foundational_services.desc: Ongoing review of ministry programs and initiatives, with the Ministry of Finance, to ensure they remain relevant, efficient and responsive to commuters within provincial budget constraints.

mandate_tags.text: In order to protect key services that British Columbians rely on, work with the Minister of Finance to review all existing Ministry of Transportation and Transit programs and initiatives to ensure our programs remain relevant, are efficient, are responsive to the needs of commuters, grow the economy, and help keep British Columbians moving

foundational_services.name: Rapid transit and major capital project delivery
foundational_services.desc: Delivery of major rapid transit capital projects, including the Broadway Subway extension to UBC and expansion of SkyTrain and RapidBus service, coordinated with TransLink, local governments and the federal government.
foundational_services.users: Internal program staff, TransLink, local governments

legislation.full_name: South Coast British Columbia Transportation Authority Act - Establishment of the transportation authority

mandate_tags.text: Identify affordable and efficient opportunities for expansion of SkyTrain, RapidBus, and rail service in the province to meet the transportation and goods movement needs of growing populations

Lead work to advance progress on the Broadway extension to UBC, including by working with the federal government, UBC, the City of Vancouver, First Nations, and all relevant government agency stakeholders

foundational_services.name: Transit safety and enforcement technology partnerships
foundational_services.desc: Works with the Ministry of Public Safety and Solicitor General, TransLink, BC Transit and local governments on technology to support safety and enforcement across the public transportation system.
foundational_services.users: Internal program staff, TransLink, BC Transit

mandate_tags.text: Support the Minister of Public Safety and Solicitor General in ensuring safety and efficiency across our public transportation system through partnerships with TransLink, BC Transit, and local governments to permit the use of technology in relation to enforcement of public transportation safety

mandate_legend.text: In order to protect key services that British Columbians rely on, work with the Minister of Finance to review all existing Ministry of Transportation and Transit programs and initiatives to ensure our programs remain relevant, are efficient, are responsive to the needs of commuters, grow the economy, and help keep British Columbians moving
